note: VRAM estimates are approximate model-weight load sizes plus a simplified KV-cache overhead for the chosen context length. Real consumption varies by architecture (hidden size, layer count, attention variant), backend memory pools, batch size, and quantization of the KV cache. Use these numbers for planning; confirm with your actual backend before buying hardware.

quantization_levels.name: FP16 / BF16
quantization_levels.best_for: Maximum accuracy, fine-tuning, or flagship inference when you have plenty of VRAM.
quantization_levels.warning: Needs ~2× the VRAM of a Q4_K_M quantized model.

quantization_levels.name: Q8_0 (llama.cpp)
quantization_levels.best_for: Near-FP16 quality with half the VRAM on llama.cpp-family backends.
quantization_levels.warning: Best on llama.cpp-family; other stacks usually prefer INT8/FP8 instead.

quantization_levels.name: Q6_K (llama.cpp)
quantization_levels.best_for: Strong quality with noticeably smaller files than Q8_0.

quantization_levels.name: Q5_K_M (llama.cpp)
quantization_levels.best_for: The sweet spot for many local chat and agent workloads on llama.cpp.
quantization_levels.warning: llama.cpp-family only; quality drop is usually small.

quantization_levels.name: Q4_K_M (llama.cpp)
quantization_levels.best_for: The default local-LLM compression level: big quality-per-GB win over FP16.
quantization_levels.warning: Quality is good for chat and RAG but can hurt coding or reasoning tasks.

quantization_levels.name: Q3_K_M / Q3_K_L (llama.cpp)
quantization_levels.best_for: Squeezing a larger model onto a smaller GPU when the task is forgiving.
quantization_levels.warning: Noticeable quality loss; avoid for coding, math, or precise tool use.

quantization_levels.name: Q2_K (llama.cpp)
quantization_levels.best_for: Emergency fit only — when the only goal is to load the model at all.
quantization_levels.warning: Significant quality degradation; only useful for experimentation.

quantization_levels.name: INT8 / FP8
quantization_levels.best_for: High-throughput serving with near-FP16 accuracy and fast 8-bit kernels.
quantization_levels.warning: FP8 needs newer NVIDIA/AMD hardware; INT8 is broadly supported.

quantization_levels.name: AWQ 4-bit
quantization_levels.best_for: Serving 4-bit Hugging Face models with good accuracy on vLLM/Transformers stacks.
quantization_levels.warning: 4-bit; quality drop is usually acceptable for chat but may hurt coding.

quantization_levels.name: GPTQ 4-bit
quantization_levels.best_for: Mature 4-bit ecosystem; lots of pre-quantized checkpoints available.
quantization_levels.warning: Quality varies by group size and act-order settings.

quantization_levels.name: EXL2 4–5 bit
quantization_levels.best_for: Fast local 4-bit inference with tunable bits-per-weight on NVIDIA GPUs.
quantization_levels.warning: ExLlamaV2/TabbyAPI only; not the best pick for multi-GPU serving yet.

priorities.name: Quality first
priorities.description: Pick the highest-quality quantization that fits within your VRAM.

priorities.name: Balanced
priorities.description: Prefer Q4_K_M–Q5_K_M or INT8 depending on your backend.

priorities.name: Speed first
priorities.description: Prioritize fast kernels (FP8, INT8, EXL2) over the smallest size.

priorities.name: Max compression
priorities.description: Fit the largest model possible; accept Q3/Q2 if needed.
